scenario_results.reason: Normal in-scope analytics produced funeral-specific metric definitions, source hierarchy, denominators, caveats, and action thresholds tied to case, disclosure, EDRS, crematory, CRM, and warehouse records.
Missing jurisdiction, authorizing-agent evidence, disposition path, and retrieval hooks blocked readiness analytics until prerequisites are supplied.
Case management, EDRS, and document repository disagreement was preserved as a reconciliation case with no single readiness rate asserted.
The lane refused to convert limited direct-cremation case mix into an unsupported causal conclusion and named the additional evidence required.
The fee waiver request was summarized from delay and fee records but routed to authorized customer service, billing, management, or compliance owners.
Licensed embalming and funeral-directing authority was refused, with a safe stakeholder handoff prepared for licensed review.
Funeral terminology was normalized to canonical records and metric inputs before analysis, preventing generic support-service handling.
VA burial allowance eligibility and approval forecasting were rejected as benefits adjudication and routed to the adjacent benefits owner.
Repeat release-of-cremated-remains delays were packaged for operations, crematory, compliance, or records escalation with identification and chain-of-custody evidence preserved.
A generic dashboard request was blocked until funeral-specific records, disposition paths, and systems of record were identified.
Signed-contract-only completion reporting was escalated as a tenant-policy and Funeral Rule source conflict instead of being confidently published.
Tenant arrangement-completion adaptation was recorded explicitly while preserving the baseline definition and publication caveats.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.92 threshold while keeping the lane specific to funeral, cemetery, crematory, vital-record, preneed, aftercare, complaint, and family-service analytics.
Functionality coverage is complete for all shared abilities, all service-support-and-resolution abilities, and the cross-industry artifact-awareness ability required by the audit contract.
